parts.answer: A description that makes reference to the following points:  test for a sulfate  result of test for a sulfate  test for a carbonate  result of test for a carbonate (1) (1) (1) (1) Tests can be in either order. Penalise unspecified acid once only in the two tests barium chloride (solution) / BaCl ((aq)) and hydrochloric acid / HCl((aq)) or barium nitrate (solution) / Ba(NO ) ((aq)) and nitric acid / HNO ((aq)) Do not award sulfuric acid / H SO white precipitate Allow solid / suspension /ppt / ppte addition of hydrochloric acid / HCl((aq)) Allow any named acid effervescence / fizzing / bubbles Allow gas given off which turns limewater cloudy Ignore just “gas” (Total for Question 1 = 10 marks) Question number Additional Guidance Mark

An answer that makes refere nce to the following points:  (add a few crystals / small amount of) phosphorus(V) chloride / phosphorus pentachloride / PCl  steamy / misty fumes (of HCl) (1) (1) Allow thionyl chloride / SOCl / Ester formation / reaction with Na Do not award PCl Allow white fumes Do not award white smoke Allow the formation of an ester M1 Addition of a carboxylic acid / named carboxylic acid and any identified strong acid Or Addition of an acyl chloride / named acyl chloride M2 Fruity / glue / characteristic smell of product Allow for addition of sodium M1 Addition of sodium / Na M2 formation of bubbles / fizzing / formation of a gas which burns with a squeaky pop Ignore just formation of gas / hydrogen Question number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to one of the following pairs of points:  bromine water / aqueous bromine / bromine solution / bromine in organic solvent / Br (aq)  orange / yellow / brown to colourless OR  potassium manganate(VII) / KMnO and sulfuric acid / H SO  purple to colourless (1) (1) (1) (1) In the results of both tests ignore “clear” Allow bromine / Br (( l)) Allow just decolourises Do not award red / red-brown with aqueous bromine but allow with bromine Allow potassium permanganate Allow acidified potassium manganate(VII) Allow just decolourises Question number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to one of the following points:  orange / yellow / brown colour still present or  purple colour remains Allow no change Allow does not decolourise Allow no reaction Allow TE on colours from b(i) Question number Additional Guidance Mark

 any two correct  third correct (1) (1) Award orange to blue three correct scores (2) any two correct scores (1) All three with the colours reversed (green to orange, green to orange and stays green) scores (1) Penalise the omission of the start colour once only Compound Colour change orange to green orange to green no change / stays orange Question number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following points:  O-H bond and 3750–3200 (cm − ) In d(i) and d(ii) penalise a single value ora range within the range once only Allow 3200-3750 (cm -1 ) Do not award –OH / -O-H Question number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following points:  C=C bond and 1669–1645 (cm − ) Allow 1669-1645 (cm -1 ) If no other mark awarded in (d)(i) and (d)(ii) allow (1) in (d)(ii) for identification of both bonds or both ranges number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes refere nce to the following points:  all three molecular ions have a m / z = 100 / all three compounds have a molar mass of 100 (g mol − ) Allow the three compounds are isomers Allow the compounds have the same molar mass Do not award the compounds have the same molecular ion number Additional Guidance Mark v ) An answer that makes reference to the following points:   CH • / CH (radical) (1) (1) Allow the + anywhere including outside of brackets around the structure Allow displayed formula Allow CH CH CH CH COH + Ignore C H O + / C H OH + Allow displayed formula Allow methyl (radical) Allow CH 3+ Allow (1) for and OH • / OH / OH + (Total for Question 2 = 12 marks) number Additional Guidance Mark

 (from) yellow  (to) orange (1) (1) Correct colours reversed scores (1) Allow peach for orange Ignore modifiers e.g. pale Do not award to red or to pink number Additional Guidance Mark  two correct values 3 4 23.05 45.1(0) 1.25 23.20 21.8(0) 21.90 number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes refere nce to the following points:  it is not concordant / not within 0.2 (cm ) Allow not within 0.1 (cm ) Allow just ‘it is rough / a trial / a rangefinder’ Do not award uncertainty e.g. ±0.1 / ±0.2 (cm ) number Additional Guidance Mark  calculation of mean titre  calculation of moles of hydrochloric acid (1) (1) Example of calculation: 21.85 + 21.80 + 21.90 = 21.85 (cm ) 21.85 x 0.200 = 0.00437 / 4.37 x 10 − (mol) Correct answer with some working scores (2) Allow TE on incorrect volumes in (b)(i) and on incorrect calculation of mean titre. Ignore SF except 1SF number Additional Guidance Mark v )  calculation of moles of barium hydroxide in 10 cm  calculation of moles of barium hydroxide in 1 dm  calculation of concentration in g dm -3 to 2 or 3 SF (1) (1) (1) Example of calculation: moles of Ba(OH) in 10 cm = ans(b)(iii) / 2 = 0.002185 / 2.185 x 10 − (mol) (ans(1)) moles of Ba(OH) in 1 dm = ans(1) x 100 = 0.2185 / 2.185 x 10 − (mol) (ans(2)) = ans(2) x 171.3 = 37.429 g = 37 / 37.4 (g dm − ) to 2 or 3 SF Do not award 37.0 Correct answer with some working scores (3) Use of 171 gives 37.4 / 37.364 ALLOW TE throughout number Additional Guidance Mark

An answer that makes refere nce to the following points:  use of a fume cupboard Allow face mask Ignore laboratory coat / goggles / gloves / well ventilated laboratory Comment: Allow respiratory equipment number Additional Guidance Mark
An answer that makes refere nce to the following points:  oxidising (agent) / oxidiser / oxidant Do not award flammable (Total for Question 3 = 11 marks) Question number Additional Guidance Mark

An answer that makes refere nce to the following points:  gives a reason for cooling  gives a reason why this ca uses a reduction in yield (1) (1) reaction is exothermic / vigorous / gives off heat / Do not award Explosive Ignore violent / quick reaction mixture may boil causing reactant / product to escape / reactant or product might evaporate or reaction may bubble / fizz / effervesce and overflow the round bottom flask (causing loss of reactant / product) Allow alkene formation / charring Ignore reference to equilibrium / rate / side reactions Do not award transfer losses number Additional Guidance Mark  anti-bumping granules Allow description of anti-bumping granules Allow other names: anti-bumping chips / beads boiling stones broken porcelain boiling chips number Additional Guidance Mark A diagram that  contains a round bottom flask with contents and any indication of heating  contains a vertical condenser with water jacket and correct water flow  is a working apparatus: not stoppered, no gaps, a joint between flask and condenser (1) (1) (1) Example of diagram: Allow apparatus unlabelled Allow pear shaped flask Allow an arrow pointing upward as an indication of heat Ignore thermometers which do not seal the apparatus M1 is available for a distillation apparatus condenser round-bottom flask water in water out number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes refere nce to the following points:  to neutralise (excess sulfuric) acid Allow to remove (excess sulfuric) acid Allow hydrobromic acid / HBr / sodium hydrogensulfate / NaHSO number Additional Guidance Mark An explanation that makes reference to the following points:  identifies the problem  gives a solution (1) (1) build-up of pressure (i n the separating funnel) Ignore causes frothing Ignore formation of CO remove the stopper (with the funnel upright) or open the tap (with funnel inverted) Note: Remove the stopper to release the pressure would score 2. Comment: mark independently number Additio

An answer that makes refere nce to the following points:  integer values of temperature in the ranges lower value 99 / 100 / 101( o C) and upper value 103 / 104 / 105( o C) Award values to nearest 0.5 o C Do not award other fractions or decimals of degrees other than nearest 0.5 o C Do not award single values even if between the two ends of the range, e.g. 102 o C number Additional Guidance Mark  reason for yield being less than 100% Example of reasons: reaction incomplete transfer losses side reactions Ignore equilibrium reached Do not award the cold water bath was not used Do not award the procedure was not followed correctly Do not award spillages Do not award impure reagents number Additional Guidance Mark  calculation of mass of 1-bromobutane required  calculation of moles of 1-bromobutane required  calculation of mass butan-1-ol required  calculation of percentage yield assumed OR  calculation of moles of butan-1-ol used  calculation of mass of 1-bromobutane for 100 % yield  calculation of volume of 1-bromobutane for 100 % yield  calculation of % yield if 20 cm prepared (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) Example of calculation: = 20 x 1.27 = 25.4 (g) = 25.4 ÷ 137 = 0.18540 (mol) = 0.18540 x 74 = 13.720 = 100 x 13.720 ÷ 21.0 = 65.332 (%) = 21 ÷ 74 = 0.2837 mol = 0.2837 x 137 = 38.878 g = 38.878 ÷ 1.27 = 30.613 (cm ) = 100 x 20 ÷ 30.613 = 65.332 (%) Do not award M4 if yield greater than 100% Use of 0.810 instead of 1.27 gives 41.699 (%) scores (3) Use of transposed M r values gives 223.9 (%) scores (2) Allow use of 136.9 Allow TE throughout Ignore SF except 1SF Correct answer with some working scores (4) (Total for Question 4 = 17 marks) Total for Paper = 50 marks Pearson Education Limited.
